title: Green Curry Paste
excerpt: Ingredients 4 – 6 green chillies 2 roughly chopped smaller onions 5 cm piece roughly chopped ginger root 2 – 4 roughly chopped garlic cloves 1 small bunch roughly chopped coriander leaves, stems and all 2 roughly chopped lemongrass stalks Zest and juice of 1 lemon 8 kaffir lime leaves, torn (or the zest of an extra lime) …
html:
Ingredients

  • 4 – 6 green chillies
  • 2 roughly chopped smaller onions
  • 5 cm piece roughly chopped ginger root
  • 2 – 4 roughly chopped garlic cloves
  • 1 small bunch roughly chopped coriander leaves, stems and all
  • 2 roughly chopped lemongrass stalks
  • Zest and juice of 1 lemon
  • 8 kaffir lime leaves, torn (or the zest of an extra lime)
  • 2.5 cm roughly chopped piece galangal (or 15 ml dried – from Woolies)
  • 15 ml roughly crushed coriander seeds
  • 15 ml roughly crushed cumin seeds
  • 15 ml roughly crushed black peppercorns
  • 30 ml Thai fish sauce (nam pla) or light soy sauce
  • 45 ml peanut or sunflower oil

Method

Remove some of the seeds from the chillies, keeping in mind that the seeds determine the heat.

Chop the chillies roughly and process together with the rest of the ingredients in your food processor until you have a smooth, thick paste.

Use the paste right away or store it in the fridge. Must be used within 3 weeks.

Enough paste for 8 servings of curry.

Easy!
